dataSources.source: FAA/ATO Aeronautical Information Services (US Airport / Heliports)

metadata.disclaimer: For planning purposes only. Data may contain errors and/or be incomplete. Binding sources are official AIP and current NOTAMs.

properties.name: Providence Hospital (ANCHORAGE, AK)
properties.remark: Hospital helipad · FAA ID: AK38 · Private (hospital — emergency medical use)
properties.conditions:
FAA does NOT mandate a specific buffer distance around uncontrolled heliports. However, per 14 CFR 107.37 (Operation near aircraft; right-of-way rules):

  'Each small unmanned aircraft must yield the right of way to all aircraft, airborne vehicles, and launch and reentry vehicles.'

Hospital helipads see frequent EMS helicopter activity that is often unscheduled and unpredictable. Maintain visual contact with helicopter operations area and yield to all helicopter movement.

properties.conditions:
FAA does NOT mandate a specific buffer distance around uncontrolled heliports. However, per 14 CFR 107.37 (Operation near aircraft; right-of-way rules):

  'Each small unmanned aircraft must yield the right of way to all aircraft, airborne vehicles, and launch and reentry vehicles.'

Hospital helipads see frequent EMS helicopter activity that is often unscheduled and unpredictable. Maintain visual contact with helicopter operations area and yield to all helicopter movement.

properties.conditions:
FAA does NOT mandate a specific buffer distance around uncontrolled heliports. However, per 14 CFR 107.37 (Operation near aircraft; right-of-way rules):

  'Each small unmanned aircraft must yield the right of way to all aircraft, airborne vehicles, and launch and reentry vehicles.'

Hospital helipads see frequent EMS helicopter activity that is often unscheduled and unpredictable. Maintain visual contact with helicopter operations area and yield to all helicopter movement.

  'Each small unmanned aircraft must yield the right of way to all aircraft, airborne vehicles, and launch and reentry vehicles.'

Hospital helipads see frequent EMS helicopter activity that is often unscheduled and unpredictable. Maintain visual contact with helicopter operations area and yield to all helicopter movement.
